agency: NOAA Fisheries SERO + Atlantic HMS Division
authority: 50 CFR Part 622 (Subparts B, Q) and 50 CFR Part 635 (HMS)
universalGearNote: Gulf EEZ reef fish (DESCEND Act + circle-hook rule, 50 CFR 622.30/31): non-stainless non-offset circle hooks with natural bait; dehooking device required; venting tool OR descending device rigged and ready.

red_snapper.open: For-hire: Jun 1 – Oct 26, 2026 · Private anglers: follow your state season
red_snapper.notes: Federal for-hire component only; captain/crew bag = 0. Private angling delegated to the Gulf states (Reef Fish Amendment 50).

lane_snapper.notes: Within the 20-fish reef-fish aggregate; no individual sub-cap.

mangrove_snapper.notes: Within the 10-fish snapper aggregate; no individual sub-cap.

mutton_snapper.notes: Sub-cap of 5 within the 10-fish snapper aggregate.

yellowtail_snapper.notes: Within the 10-fish snapper aggregate; no individual sub-cap.

red_grouper.open: Year-round, except closed Feb 1 – Mar 31, 2026 seaward of the ~20-fathom contour
red_grouper.notes: Within the 4-fish grouper aggregate; max 2 red grouper/person/day; captain/crew bag = 0.

gag_grouper.notes: Within the 4-fish grouper aggregate; captain/crew bag = 0. 2026 fall opening dates not yet announced (2025 was Sep 1–15).

black_grouper.open: Year-round, except closed Feb 1 – Mar 31, 2026 seaward of the ~20-fathom contour
black_grouper.notes: Within the 4-fish grouper aggregate; captain/crew bag = 0.

scamp.open: Year-round, except closed Feb 1 – Mar 31, 2026 seaward of the ~20-fathom contour
scamp.notes: Within the 4-fish grouper aggregate (Other Shallow-Water Grouper complex); captain/crew bag = 0.

greater_amberjack.open: Closed Jan 1 – Aug 31, 2026; reopens Sep 1, 2026 (subject to in-season closure)
greater_amberjack.notes: Captain/crew bag = 0. 2025 in-season closure carried through Aug 31, 2026.

lesser_amberjack.notes: Slot 14–22 in; 5-fish aggregate combined with banded rudderfish.

almaco_jack.notes: Within the 20-fish reef-fish aggregate; no federal minimum size.

banded_rudderfish.notes: Slot 14–22 in; 5-fish aggregate combined with lesser amberjack.

spanish_mackerel.notes: Gulf migratory group; fishing year Apr 1 – Mar 31; in-season accountability if ACL reached.

cero_mackerel.open: Not federally managed in Gulf — follow state rules
cero_mackerel.notes: Removed from the federal Coastal Migratory Pelagics FMP (Amendment 18, 2012). State regulations apply in state waters.

yellowfin_tuna.notes: HMS Angling or Charter/Headboat permit required aboard. Cannot retain if a hammerhead or oceanic whitetip shark is on board.

blackfin_tuna.open: Not federally managed — follow state rules
blackfin_tuna.notes: Not among federally managed Atlantic HMS species. State regulations may apply in state waters.

blue_marlin.notes: No per-person/day limit; US-wide annual cap of 250 blue+white marlin + roundscale spearfish. HMS permit required; mandatory landing report within 24 hr via hmspermits.noaa.gov.

sailfish.notes: HMS permit required; mandatory reporting; not counted in the 250-marlin cap.

white_marlin.notes: No per-person/day limit; within the 250-marlin annual cap. HMS permit; mandatory landing report within 24 hr.

gray_triggerfish.open: Open — closed Jan 1 – Feb 28 & Jun 1 – Jul 31, 2026 (subject to in-season closure)
gray_triggerfish.notes: Sub-cap of 1 within the 20-fish reef-fish aggregate; captain/crew bag = 0.

blacktip_shark.notes: 1 per vessel/trip in the 'all other sharks' group. HMS permit + shark endorsement; non-offset non-stainless circle hooks required when fishing for sharks. Cannot retain if a hammerhead or oceanic whitetip shark is on board.

cobia.notes: Gulf migratory group; per-person limit 1, per-vessel limit 2 (Amendment 32). Land intact.

wahoo.open: Not federally managed in Gulf — follow state rules
wahoo.notes: Managed under the Atlantic Dolphin-Wahoo FMP (Atlantic federal waters only). No federal Gulf EEZ regulation; state rules apply.

mahi.open: Not federally managed in Gulf — follow state rules
mahi.notes: Managed under the Atlantic Dolphin-Wahoo FMP (Atlantic only); removed from Gulf CMP FMP (Amendment 18, 2012). No federal Gulf EEZ regulation; state rules apply.
